proves: Enterprise procurement missing documents and release holds are explicit.
Privacy, retention, support/SLA, incident, and external-share approval gates are ready for owner review.
Draft procurement artifacts are separated from security proof and buyer-shareable claims.
does_not_prove: Privacy compliance, DPA approval, or legal review.
Enterprise SLA, support readiness, incident-response readiness, or breach-notification readiness.
RLS/tenant isolation proof, hosted runtime proof, AI runtime security proof, buyer validation, or prediction accuracy.

current_source_alignment.implication: Privacy should be treated as enterprise risk management with clear data, retention, and processing evidence.
Procurement buyers should receive direct answers about secure-by-design practices, current proof, and supplier accountability.
Secure-development, vulnerability response, and release evidence need repeatable artifacts, not generic claims.
AI trust claims should remain governed, measured, and managed across the lifecycle.
AI management-system language is valid only as alignment unless an audited/certified management system exists.

required_documents.title: Privacy notice or buyer data-processing summary
required_documents.why_required: Enterprise buyers need to know what data is collected, why, where it is stored, and who can access it.

required_documents.title: Data inventory and classification table
required_documents.expected_artifact: owner-approved data inventory mapped to RLS classifications
required_documents.why_required: Privacy, retention, RLS, and support claims need a shared data-category map.

required_documents.title: Retention and deletion policy
required_documents.why_required: Procurement reviewers ask how long buyer data is retained and how deletion requests are handled.

required_documents.title: DPA and subprocessor position
required_documents.expected_artifact: DPA/subprocessor packet or explicit pilot-specific exception
required_documents.why_required: Public-sector and enterprise procurement typically require data-processing and subprocessor terms.

required_documents.title: Pilot support and SLA terms
required_documents.expected_artifact: support owner, response target, uptime boundary, escalation path, and maintenance window
required_documents.why_required: A paid pilot needs clear support responsibilities even before full enterprise SLA commitments.

required_documents.title: Incident response and breach-notice stance
required_documents.expected_artifact: incident triage, severity, notification, and communication runbook
required_documents.why_required: Security reviewers need to know how incidents are detected, triaged, and communicated.

required_documents.title: Secure SDLC and vulnerability response
required_documents.expected_artifact: release checklist, vulnerability intake, patch target, dependency scan cadence
required_documents.why_required: Secure-by-demand procurement asks how suppliers build, test, and remediate software.

required_documents.title: External-share approval register
required_documents.expected_artifact: owner-approved list of questionnaire rows and artifacts that may be shared with buyers
required_documents.why_required: Procurement packets must avoid leaking secrets, internal logs, or unsupported claims.

external_share_artifacts.share_boundary: Internal readiness map unless owner marks external-shareable rows.
Can show remediation path; cannot claim deployed RLS proof.
Can show RLS evidence validation status; cannot claim tenant isolation unless validator and owner-approved linked evidence pass.
Can show smoke plan; cannot claim hosted proof until logs/screenshots are attached.
Can show local red-team readiness; hosted runtime resistance remains unproven.
Draft action policy only; owner approval and hosted boundary tests are missing.
First-sale proof gate only; no buyer validation claim.
Accuracy claim governance only; no real resolved-outcome accuracy proof.

acceptance_gates.required_evidence: Owner evidence register validates required documents, owner approvals, artifact paths, external-share approvals, no-secrets markers, and no unsupported claim markers.
acceptance_gates.allowed_claim: Enterprise evidence register is ready for procurement review.
acceptance_gates.prohibited_claim: Draft documents or unvalidated rows prove enterprise readiness.

acceptance_gates.required_evidence: Owner-approved data inventory, data categories, processing purpose, storage location, access roles, retention, and deletion path.
acceptance_gates.allowed_claim: Privacy/data inventory is a required procurement gate.

acceptance_gates.required_evidence: Pilot support owner, response targets, escalation path, maintenance boundary, incident severity model, and breach/notice stance.
acceptance_gates.allowed_claim: Support/SLA terms are pending owner approval.
acceptance_gates.prohibited_claim: Enterprise SLA, production support, or incident-response readiness.

acceptance_gates.required_evidence: Owner marks which rows/artifacts are external-shareable and removes secrets/internal-only detail.
acceptance_gates.allowed_claim: Procurement packet draft exists for internal review.

acceptance_gates.required_evidence: Owner-approved RLS migration, pgTAP/linked Supabase proof, hosted smoke logs/screenshots, and deploy binding.
acceptance_gates.allowed_claim: RLS and hosted proof path exists.
acceptance_gates.prohibited_claim: Tenant isolation or hosted runtime is proven.

acceptance_gates.required_evidence: Hosted LLM red-team fixtures, owner-approved high-impact action policy, hosted no-autonomous-action tests, and redacted logs.
acceptance_gates.prohibited_claim: Hosted AI security, no-autonomous-action, or public-sector AI trust is proven.

acceptance_gates.required_evidence: Buyer commitment signal plus resolved-outcome forecast evidence before enterprise-ready or world-class prediction claims.
acceptance_gates.allowed_claim: Guided pilot posture remains the correct commercial boundary.
acceptance_gates.prohibited_claim: Enterprise-ready, buyer-validated, or world-class prediction claims.

release_holds.evidence_needed: Validated owner evidence register with required documents, owner approvals, external-share status, no-secrets markers, and no unsupported claim markers.
Owner-approved privacy/data-processing summary, retention/deletion terms, DPA/subprocessor stance, and data inventory.
Pilot support/SLA terms, escalation owner, incident response, and breach/notice stance.
Owner-approved external-share register for procurement artifacts and questionnaire rows.
RLS proof validator must pass with owner-approved local and linked pgTAP rows plus hosted smoke evidence.
Hosted LLM security and no-autonomous-action proof.

required_owner_inputs: Fill and validate docs/launch-readiness/enterprise-procurement-evidence-register-2026-06-06.csv before upgrading enterprise procurement claims.
Approve or provide privacy/data-processing summary, retention/deletion policy, DPA/subprocessor stance, and data inventory.
Approve or provide pilot support/SLA, incident response, escalation owner, and breach/notice stance.
Mark which procurement questionnaire rows and artifacts can be shared externally.
Run RLS, hosted smoke, hosted LLM, and hosted AI action-boundary proof after owner-approved environment access.
Fill and validate the RLS proof evidence register after local and linked pgTAP execution before any tenant-isolation claim is upgraded.
Keep enterprise-ready, public-sector AI trust, and certification claims blocked until proof and approvals exist.
next_commands_after_owner_approval: Attach owner-approved privacy/support/SLA/incident documents or update this gate with their paths.

checklist.required_evidence: Owner evidence register validates required documents, owner approvals, artifact paths, external-share approvals, no-secrets markers, and no unsupported claim markers.
checklist.allowed_claim: Enterprise evidence register is ready for procurement review.
checklist.prohibited_claim: Draft documents or unvalidated rows prove enterprise readiness.

checklist.allowed_claim: Privacy/data inventory is a required procurement gate.

checklist.allowed_claim: Support/SLA terms are pending owner approval.
checklist.prohibited_claim: Enterprise SLA, production support, or incident-response readiness.

checklist.required_evidence: Owner marks which rows/artifacts are external-shareable and removes secrets/internal-only detail.
checklist.allowed_claim: Procurement packet draft exists for internal review.

checklist.required_evidence: Owner-approved RLS migration, pgTAP/linked Supabase proof, hosted smoke logs/screenshots, and deploy binding.
checklist.allowed_claim: RLS and hosted proof path exists.
checklist.prohibited_claim: Tenant isolation or hosted runtime is proven.

checklist.required_evidence: Hosted LLM red-team fixtures, owner-approved high-impact action policy, hosted no-autonomous-action tests, and redacted logs.
checklist.prohibited_claim: Hosted AI security, no-autonomous-action, or public-sector AI trust is proven.

checklist.required_evidence: Buyer commitment signal plus resolved-outcome forecast evidence before enterprise-ready or world-class prediction claims.
checklist.allowed_claim: Guided pilot posture remains the correct commercial boundary.
checklist.prohibited_claim: Enterprise-ready, buyer-validated, or world-class prediction claims.
